title: EVA: Efficient Reinforcement Learning for End-to-End Video Agent
task: Efficient video understanding via planning-before-perception
key_results: 6-12% improvement over MLLM baselines, 1-3% gain over prior adaptive methods on six video benchmarks
comments: Three-stage pipeline (SFT→KTO→GRPO) is novel but adds complexity; unclear how much improvement comes from each stage versus the planning-first strategy.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Planning-before-perception enables more efficient video understanding than perception-first strategies
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via 6-12% improvement over perception-first baselines

title: T-MAP: Red-Teaming LLM Agents with Trajectory-aware Evolutionary Search
task: Red-teaming LLM agents via trajectory-aware evolutionary search
key_results: Substantially higher attack realization rate (ARR) than baselines; effective against GPT-5.2, Gemini-3-Pro, Qwen3.5, GLM-5
comments: First work to focus on multi-step tool execution vulnerabilities in MCP; methodology is sound but exact ARR numbers not disclosed in abstract.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Trajectory-aware search better discovers agent vulnerabilities than prior text-only red-teaming
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; T-MAP outperforms baselines in ARR across multiple frontier models

title: UI-Voyager: A Self-Evolving GUI Agent Learning via Failed Experience
task: Mobile GUI automation via self-evolving agent with failed experience learning
key_results: 4B model achieves 81.0% Pass@1 success rate on AndroidWorld, exceeding human performance
comments: GRSD's identification of fork points is clever but requires group rollouts, adding computational cost; strong empirical results warrant attention.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Self-evolution via failed trajectory correction outperforms sparse reward learning
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; RFT+GRSD achieves 81% success, surpassing baselines and human performance

title: Why Does Self-Distillation (Sometimes) Degrade the Reasoning Capability of LLMs?
task: Understanding self-distillation degradation in mathematical reasoning
key_results: Performance drops up to 40% in mathematical reasoning; suppression of epistemic verbalization identified as root cause
comments: Well-designed controlled experiments; finding is important but applies primarily to reasoning tasks—effectiveness may differ for other domains.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Conditioning teacher on rich context suppresses uncertainty, harming OOD reasoning performance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; degradation up to 40% observed; uncertainty expression crucial for robust reasoning

title: GameplayQA: A Benchmarking Framework for Decision-Dense POV-Synced Multi-Video Understanding of 3D Virtual Agents
task: Multi-video understanding and reasoning for autonomous agents in 3D environments
key_results: Benchmarks MLLMs with 2.4K diagnostic QA pairs at 1.22 labels/second; reveals substantial gap from human performance
comments: GameplayQA's dense annotation and triadic decomposition (Self/Other/World) are valuable; benchmark is well-structured but limited to gameplay settings.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Current MLLMs struggle with temporal grounding, agent attribution, and decision density in multi-agent environments
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; common failure modes identified through diagnostic QA evaluation

title: When Models Judge Themselves: Unsupervised Self-Evolution for Multimodal Reasoning
task: Unsupervised self-evolution for multimodal reasoning without human annotations
key_results: Consistent improvements on five mathematical reasoning benchmarks using only self-consistency signal and GRPO
comments: Novel use of group-relative scores to convert absolute to relative advantages is elegant; scalability claims strong but limited to mathematical domains.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Self-consistency signal enables stable self-evolution without external reward models or human annotations
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; group relative policy optimization achieves improvements without supervision

title: CarePilot: A Multi-Agent Framework for Long-Horizon Computer Task Automation in Healthcare
task: Long-horizon computer task automation in healthcare systems
key_results: CarePilot outperforms closed-source/open-source baselines by ~15.26% and 3.38% on CareFlow benchmark
comments: Actor-critic with dual memory is well-motivated; CareFlow benchmark valuable for healthcare domain but limited scope (4 application types).

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dual-memory (long-term + short-term) with iterative refinement improves long-horizon medical workflow automation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; CarePilot achieves SOTA on healthcare task benchmark

title: 4DGS360: 360° Gaussian Reconstruction of Dynamic Objects from a Single Video
task: 360° dynamic object reconstruction from monocular video
key_results: SOTA on iPhone360, iPhone, DAVIS datasets; robust to 135° separation between training and test views
comments: 3D-native initialization via AnchorTAP3D is principled; iPhone360 benchmark is useful but relatively small scale.

hypotheses.hypothesis: 3D-native initialization mitigates 2D-native prior overfitting and improves occluded region consistency
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SOTA results on 360° reconstruction with large view separation

title: CUA-Suite: Massive Human-annotated Video Demonstrations for Computer-Use Agents
task: Providing large-scale video demonstrations for computer-use agents
key_results: VideoCUA: ~10K tasks, 55 hours continuous video, 6M frames; GroundCUA: 56K screenshots, 3.6M UI annotations; current models ~60% failure rate
comments: CUA-Suite is a major resource contribution; 55 hours of continuous video is transformative for the field but still modest compared to internet scale.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Continuous video with temporal dynamics is critical for scaling computer-use agents beyond sparse screenshots
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via dataset design; high failure rate on professional apps suggests this is a key bottleneck

title: Toward Physically Consistent Driving Video World Models under Challenging Trajectories
task: Physically consistent driving video generation under challenging trajectories
key_results: PhyGenesis outperforms SOTA especially on challenging trajectories; trained with physics-rich heterogeneous dataset (real + CARLA)
comments: Physical condition generator and physics-enhanced decoder are well-motivated; CARLA simulation augmentation is clever but may introduce domain bias.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Learning from challenging trajectories in simulation improves physical consistency under counterfactual conditions
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; CARLA-augmented training enables trajectory correction and consistent generation

title: OmniWeaving: Towards Unified Video Generation with Free-form Composition and Reasoning
task: Unified omni-level video generation with multimodal composition and reasoning
key_results: OmniWeaving SOTA among open-source unified models; handles diverse compositional and reasoning-augmented scenarios
comments: Ambitious scope (text, multi-image, video inputs) but IntelligentVBench is newly proposed—independent validation needed.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Reasoning-informed multimodal composition enables sophisticated unified video generation
hypotheses.result: Claimed; SOTA on newly proposed IntelligentVBench but limited external validation

title: Can LLM Agents Be CFOs? A Benchmark for Resource Allocation in Dynamic Enterprise Environments
task: Evaluating LLM agents on long-horizon resource allocation under uncertainty
key_results: EnterpriseArena benchmark with 132-month simulator; only 16% of runs survive full horizon; larger models don't reliably outperform smaller
comments: Excellent benchmark design with partial observability and budgeted tools; finding that scale doesn't guarantee performance is valuable.

hypotheses.hypothesis: LLM agents struggle with long-horizon resource allocation under uncertainty, a distinct capability gap
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 84% failure rate and poor scaling with model size

title: Unleashing Spatial Reasoning in Multimodal Large Language Models via Textual Representation Guided Reasoning
task: 3D spatial reasoning in multimodal models via textual representation
key_results: TRACE prompting yields consistent improvements on VSI-Bench and OST-Bench across diverse MLLM backbones
comments: Cognitive theory motivation (allocentric reasoning) is well-grounded; TRACE is a prompting method rather than architectural change, limiting impact.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Intermediate text-based 3D representations improve spatial reasoning in egocentric video
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; TRACE yields consistent improvements across MLLM scales and architectures

title: StreamingClaw Technical Report
task: Unified streaming video understanding and embodied intelligence
key_results: StreamingClaw integrates real-time reasoning, multimodal memory, and closed-loop perception-decision-action; OpenClaw-compatible
comments: Ambitious framework combining five core capabilities; technical report format limits quantitative validation—benchmarks unclear.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Unified streaming architecture enables proactive embodied intelligence with long-term memory
hypotheses.result: Not quantitatively validated in abstract; design principles sound but evaluation lacking

title: 6Bit-Diffusion: Inference-Time Mixed-Precision Quantization for Video Diffusion Models
task: Inference-time mixed-precision quantization for video diffusion models
key_results: 1.92× end-to-end acceleration, 3.32× memory reduction; NVFP4/INT8 mixed-precision with temporal delta cache
comments: Linear correlation between input-output difference and quantization sensitivity is insightful; temporal delta cache exploits redundancy well.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Adaptive precision allocation based on block sensitivity and temporal redundancy enables aggressive quantization
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 1.92× speedup and 3.32× memory reduction without quality loss

title: UniFunc3D: Unified Active Spatial-Temporal Grounding for 3D Functionality Segmentation
task: Spatial-temporal grounding for 3D functionality segmentation
key_results: UniFunc3D achieves 59.9% mIoU improvement on SceneFun3D, surpassing training-free and training-based methods; training-free
comments: Active spatial-temporal grounding with coarse-to-fine strategy is elegant; impressive gains but limited to single benchmark.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Joint spatial-temporal reasoning with adaptive frame selection enables fine-grained functionality grounding
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 59.9% mIoU improvement with training-free approach

title: LagerNVS: Latent Geometry for Fully Neural Real-time Novel View Synthesis
task: Real-time novel view synthesis with latent 3D features
key_results: SOTA deterministic NVS (31.4 PSNR on Re10k), real-time inference, generalizes to in-the-wild data
comments: 3D-aware latent features pre-trained on explicit 3D supervision is sound; lightweight decoder enables real-time performance.

hypotheses.hypothesis: 3D-aware latent features from pre-trained 3D supervision improve feed-forward NVS performance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SOTA PSNR and real-time inference on multiple benchmarks

title: PLDR-LLMs Reason At Self-Organized Criticality
task: Understanding reasoning in LLMs via self-organized criticality
key_results: LLMs trained at criticality exhibit reasoning; order parameter derived from model weights predicts reasoning capability without benchmark evaluation
comments: Novel theoretical framework inspired by physics phase transitions; claims are bold but validation relies on existing benchmarks, not independent testing.

hypotheses.hypothesis: LLMs trained at self-organized criticality exhibit reasoning behavior analogous to phase transitions
hypotheses.result: Partially supported; benchmark scores correlate with criticality but mechanism unproven

title: The NPC1/USP7/p53 axis regulates cholesterol and promotes the proliferation of hepatocellular carcinoma
task: Establish NPC1-USP7-p53 axis linking cholesterol metabolism to HCC proliferation
key_results: NPC1 upregulated in HCC tissues and associated with poor prognosis; NPC1 silencing suppresses proliferation in vitro and in vivo; NPC1 disrupts USP7-p53 binding, enhancing p53 degradation; NPC1 modulates cholesterol via p53-SREBP2 axis
comments: Elegant dual-mechanism model linking lysosomal cholesterol transport, p53 degradation, and HCC growth; identifies actionable therapeutic vulnerabilities.

hypotheses.hypothesis: NPC1 promotes HCC by disrupting USP7-p53 interaction
hypotheses.result: NPC1 enhances p53 ubiquitination and proteasomal degradation

hypotheses.hypothesis: NPC1 modulates cholesterol metabolism in p53-dependent manner
hypotheses.result: NPC1-mediated cholesterol reduction reversed by p53 knockdown

title: The oncogenic control of nucleotide synthesis
task: Review oncogenic control of nucleotide synthesis in cancer
key_results: TP53 loss, LKB1 loss, PI3K-AKT-mTORC1, MYC, and RAS coordinate nucleotide biosynthesis; integration of glycolysis, pentose-phosphate pathway, folate-dependent one-carbon metabolism, and glutamine/aspartate provisioning
comments: Comprehensive mechanistic review organizing complex metabolic reprogramming into actionable therapeutic model; nucleotide metabolism represents central nexus of oncogenic signaling.

inputs: oncogenic lesions (TP53 loss, LKB1 loss, PI3K-AKT-mTORC1, MYC, RAS)
outputs: de novo purine and pyrimidine synthesis rates

hypotheses.hypothesis: Tumors selectively activate de novo versus salvage nucleotide synthesis based on environmental context
hypotheses.result: Environment-shaped routing model explains metabolic plasticity and therapy response

title: Moonlighting cytosolic function of ACAD9: suppression of TRAF6-mediated osteoclastogenesis and protection against osteoporosis
task: Identify cytosolic ACAD9 function in suppressing osteoclast differentiation
key_results: ACAD9 blocks TRAF6-mediated K63-polyubiquitination and promotes K48-linked degradation; Acad9 knockout mice show increased osteoclast numbers and decreased bone mass
comments: Reveals moonlighting function of a mitochondrial protein in cytosolic signaling, with clear therapeutic relevance to osteoporosis.

hypotheses.hypothesis: ACAD9 suppresses TRAF6 signaling by competitive binding
hypotheses.result: ACAD9 prevents UBC13/UEV1A interaction with TRAF6

hypotheses.hypothesis: ACAD9 degrades TRAF6 via proteasomal pathway
hypotheses.result: K48-linked polyubiquitination and proteasomal degradation confirmed

title: Polystyrene nanoparticles promote endometrial cancer development through the ACSS2-mediated reprogramming of arachidonic acid metabolism
task: Elucidate polystyrene nanoplastic promotion of endometrial cancer via ACSS2-arachidonic acid metabolism
key_results: PS-NPs accumulate in endometrial cells and organoids; AMPK activates ACSS2 and promotes nuclear translocation; nuclear ACSS2 increases H3K9 acetylation and PLA2G3 expression; elevated arachidonic acid drives EMT
comments: Novel environmental health mechanism linking nanoplastic exposure to cancer progression; ACSS2 emerges as key metabolic-epigenetic node but clinical relevance requires validation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: PS-NPs activate AMPK-ACSS2-PLA2G3 axis to promote EMT
hypotheses.result: ACSS2 nuclear translocation increases H3K9 acetylation and arachidonic acid production

title: Bleb-based extravasation uses conserved morphodynamics but divergent calcium control
task: Identify conserved morphodynamics but divergent Ca²⁺ control in cell extravasation
key_results: All extravasating cells form Ca²⁺-dependent membrane blebs; PGCs and HT-1080 use store-operated Ca²⁺ entry (SOCE); epithelial-derived PC-3 and MDA-MB-231 use IP₃R-mediated ER release
comments: Reveals convergent phenotype (blebbing) driven by divergent molecular mechanisms; evolutionary mapping to ancestral and metazoan pathways provides conceptual framework.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Bleb-based extravasation is morphodynamically conserved but uses distinct Ca²⁺ pathways
hypotheses.result: PGCs/HT-1080 use SOCE; epithelial-derived cancers use IP₃R pathway

title: Toolbox of FRET-based c-di-GMP biosensors and its FRET-To-Sort application for genome-wide mapping of c-di-GMP regulation
task: Map genome-wide c-di-GMP regulation using FRET biosensors and cell sorting
key_results: Large toolkit of FRET biosensors with diverse binding affinities; FRET-To-Sort identified flagellar genes affecting c-di-GMP levels; inhibited flagellar rotation reduces c-di-GMP biosynthesis via proton motive force
comments: Novel methodological approach combining FRET with transposon screening; connection between motility and signaling metabolism provides mechanistic insight.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Flagellar rotation modulates c-di-GMP biosynthesis
hypotheses.result: Inhibited flagellar rotation increases proton motive force and reduces c-di-GMP

title: Lactylation at lysine 145 fosters KAT8-TIP60 complex formation to promote p53 acetylation at lysine 120 and its pro-apoptotic function
task: Establish KAT8 K145 lactylation as regulator of p53 pro-apoptotic function and doxorubicin cardiotoxicity
key_results: GCN5 catalyzes KAT8 K145 lactylation; SIRT6 delactylates; K145 lactylation promotes KAT8-TIP60 complex formation and p53 K120 acetylation; doxorubicin increases K145 lactylation; glimepiride mitigates doxorubicin-induced cardiomyocyte apoptosis
comments: Links emerging lactylation PTM to p53 activation and defines mechanism of anthracycline cardiotoxicity; glimepiride repurposing offers translational opportunity.

hypotheses.hypothesis: KAT8 K145 lactylation drives p53 pro-apoptotic function
hypotheses.result: K145 lactylation enhances KAT8-TIP60 complex and p53 K120 acetylation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Glimepiride inhibits KAT8 lactylation and mitigates doxorubicin cardiotoxicity
hypotheses.result: Glimepiride disrupts GCN5-KAT8 interaction and reduces p53 activation in cardiomyocytes

title: Role of dysregulated calcium homeostasis in astrocytes in neurodegenerative disorders
task: Review dysregulated astrocyte Ca²⁺ homeostasis in neurodegenerative disease pathophysiology
key_results: Astrocyte Ca²⁺ signaling progressively remodeled during aging and dysregulated in Alzheimer, Parkinson, Huntington, and ALS; alterations heterogeneous and context-dependent; Ca²⁺ dysregulation compromises homeostatic functions and promotes neuronal vulnerability
comments: Comprehensive mechanistic review establishing astrocytic Ca²⁺ as central node in neurodegeneration; identifies broad therapeutic opportunity but requires cell-type-specific targeting strategies.

model_io.model: Astrocyte Ca²⁺ signaling in health and disease

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dysregulated astrocyte Ca²⁺ signaling drives neuronal circuit dysfunction
hypotheses.result: Ca²⁺ alterations compromise homeostatic functions and increase neuronal vulnerability

title: Dysferlin stabilizes membrane nanodomains of cardiomyocytes after myocardial infarction
task: Determine dysferlin role in preserving sarcolemmal nanodomains after myocardial infarction
key_results: Dysferlin upregulated 230% in WT MI border zone; dysferlin-KO mice show larger infarcts and reduced systolic function; dysferlin clusters at TAT structures and connexin-43 plaques
comments: Integrates proteomics with super-resolution microscopy to reveal protein stabilization function; dysferlin emerges as therapeutic target for post-MI cardiomyocyte protection.

model_io.model: Left anterior descending artery ligation in mice

inputs: cardiac tissue from infarct, border, remote zones

outputs: dysferlin interaction partners at TAT and ICD

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dysferlin stabilizes sarcolemmal nanodomains in MI border zone
hypotheses.result: Dysferlin clusters at TAT and ICD, protecting membrane architecture

title: Multimerin1 and not Galectin-8 tempers WNT signaling to promote gastric chief cell differentiation
task: Identify driver of gastric chief cell differentiation independent of galectin-8
key_results: Lgals8–/– phenotype (delayed differentiation) attributed to deletions of Mmrn1 and Snca, not Lgals8; Mmrn1–/–/Snca–/– mice show hyperproliferative corpus with elevated nuclear β-catenin; multimerin-1 produced by endothelial cells tempers WNT signaling
comments: Reveals mesodermal-epithelial cross-talk in gastric development; unexpected finding that key phenotype arose from genetic background highlights importance of pure knockout validation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multimerin-1 from endothelial cells suppresses WNT signaling in gastric epithelium
hypotheses.result: Mmrn1–/–/Snca–/– mice show elevated β-catenin and hyperproliferation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Galectin-8 plays no role in chief cell homeostasis
hypotheses.result: Pure Lgals8 knockout needed to test; phenotype was due to linked deletions

title: Mesenchymal to epithelial transition (MET) in cancer progression: insights from logical modeling
task: Model MET dynamics and constraints using logical network of EMT/MET inducers
key_results: MET is highly dependent on mesenchymal context; EMT program persistence constrains MET inducers; perturbing one EMT pathway reinforces alternatives, maintaining hybrid states
comments: Systems-level perspective reveals MET as context-dependent and constrained by EMT program redundancy; highlights importance of hybrid epithelial-mesenchymal states in cancer biology.

hypotheses.hypothesis: MET induction depends on mesenchymal context
hypotheses.result: In silico simulations show context-dependent MET trajectories along epithelial-mesenchymal spectrum

hypotheses.hypothesis: EMT program redundancy prevents full MET reversion
hypotheses.result: Perturbing one EMT pathway activates alternative pathways, maintaining hybrid states

title: Ultrastructural and histochemical insights into neotenic and metamorphic axolotl lungs with clues to pulmonary regeneration
task: Characterize ultrastructural and histochemical changes in axolotl lungs during metamorphosis
key_results: Metamorphic lungs show expanded air spaces, dense elastic fibers, and mature lamellar bodies; neotenic lungs show thick alveolar walls and prominent collagen deposition
comments: Provides morphological framework for vertebrate lung adaptation but notes axolotl histology differs from mammalian; relevance to human pulmonary regeneration requires further validation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Thyroid hormone induces lung restructuring for terrestrial adaptation
hypotheses.result: Metamorphic lungs show expanded air spaces and mature pneumocyte ultrastructure

title: Rapamycin reduces peritendinous fibrosis but has a limited effect on intratendinous healing in a rodent Achilles tendon injury model
task: Evaluate rapamycin efficacy in reducing tendon fibrosis and promoting Achilles tendon healing
key_results: Rapamycin decreased peritendinous fibrosis and modulated microRNA levels; did not enhance intratendinous healing or lesion repair within 3 weeks post-injury
comments: Negative result appropriately documented; rapamycin shows partial benefit (reduced fibrosis) but insufficient for clinical therapeutic efficacy in acute tendon injury model.

model_io.model: Needle-induced Achilles tendon injury in rats

hypotheses.hypothesis: mTOR inhibition enhances tendon healing via modulation of resident cell populations
hypotheses.result: Rapamycin reduced peritendinous fibrosis but did not enhance core tendon healing

title: ADME/drug-likeness and functional properties of Punica granatum seeds supported with molecular docking, GC-MS and LC-MS/MS analysis
task: Assess phytochemical composition, drug-likeness, and bioactivity of Punica granatum seed extract
key_results: Major constituents: 2-oxatricyclo[4.3.1.0(3,8)]decane, 2-heptenal, gallic acid, ellagic acid; anti-genotoxicity 53.31–74.48%; anti-proliferation 15.6%; α-glucosidase inhibition 30.2–61.3%; AChE inhibition up to 67.8%; logP <5 for all compounds (Lipinski compliance)
comments: Comprehensive phytochemical-bioactivity-pharmacokinetics correlation; in vitro activities and favorable drug-like properties suggest potential utility, though in vivo validation lacking.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Gallic acid and ellagic acid phenolic derivatives mediate bioactivity
hypotheses.result: Major phenolic constituents show favorable drug-likeness and docking interactions

title: Endometrial regeneration with mesenchymal stem cells and exosomes: an experimental rat model of intrauterine adhesions
task: Compare MSC and exosome treatments for endometrial regeneration in intrauterine adhesions
key_results: All treated groups reduced collagen deposition vs positive controls; Adipose-Exo group showed most favorable remodeling scores; IV and local UC-Exo delivery produced comparable effects
comments: Direct comparison of cell versus acellular therapies and delivery routes is valuable, though histological improvements do not guarantee functional restoration of fertility.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Exosomes are as effective as MSCs for endometrial regeneration
hypotheses.result: Exosome groups showed comparable or superior remodeling to MSC groups

hypotheses.hypothesis: Tissue source influences regeneration profile
hypotheses.result: Adipose-Exo group most favorable; UC and adipose sources differ in efficacy

title: Leveraging weighted embedding and Transformer architecture to improve phenotype prediction of complex traits for crops
task: Deep learning phenotypic prediction of complex traits in crops using GWAS-weighted embeddings
key_results: 77.5% improvement in prediction accuracy vs. baselines, 78% MSE reduction, 1.8-2.4× computational efficiency gain
comments: Weighted embedding mechanism incorporates GWAS priors effectively; biological interpretability through variant attribution is valuable for crop breeding.

hypotheses.hypothesis: GWAS-derived SNP weighting + Transformer multi-head attention capture long-range genomic dependencies
hypotheses.result: Outperformed 7 baseline models across 6 datasets with 77.5% accuracy improvement

title: Completely resolved structural variants by optical genome mapping with adaptive sampling from CNV discovery
task: Comprehensive structural variant detection combining optical genome mapping and long-read sequencing
key_results: Undetected SVs revealed in 14/30 cases (46.7%); gene disruptions explaining clinical features in 7/30 (23.3%)
comments: Integration of orthogonal methods substantially improves diagnostic resolution; adaptive sampling strategy effectively targets complex breakpoints.

model_io.model: Optical genome mapping (OGM) with ultra-high MW DNA labeling
Oxford Nanopore long-read sequencing with adaptive sampling

hypotheses.hypothesis: OGM detects SVs missed by exome-seq alone
hypotheses.result: 46.7% of cases revealed previously undetected SVs

hypotheses.hypothesis: Combining OGM + long-read sequencing resolves complex multi-segment SVs
hypotheses.result: Complex SVs with numerous breakpoints resolved efficiently

title: Integrated single-cell transcriptomic atlas of human gastric and colorectal tissues across diverse phenotypes
task: Comprehensive single-cell transcriptomic atlas of gastric and colorectal tissues
key_results: 574,532 stomach cells and 479,629 colorectal cells across 70 cell types each, 229 stomach and 220 colorectal tissue samples
comments: Resource paper providing curated reference atlas; utility depends on downstream analytical studies leveraging this foundation.

model_io.model: Single-cell RNA-seq workflow with computational validation

title: Fractional-order epidemic modeling with a deep neural network framework
task: Fractional-order SITR epidemic modeling with deep neural network surrogate
key_results: BR-DNN solutions in high agreement with Adams-Bashforth-Moulton numerical solutions; validated via MSE, regression analysis, absolute error plots
comments: Fractional-order model captures memory-dependent disease dynamics; BR-DNN provides efficient computational alternative with independent validation.

model_io.model: Fractional-order SITR with Caputo derivative and saturated incidence
outputs: Susceptible, Infectious, Treated, Recovered populations over time

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fractional-order (α < 1) captures long-term memory effects absent in integer-order models
hypotheses.result: Numerical experiments demonstrated importance of memory effects on disease transmission/treatment dynamics

hypotheses.hypothesis: BR-DNN provides accurate data-driven surrogate for fractional ODE solutions
hypotheses.result: High agreement between BR-DNN and Adams-Bashforth-Moulton solutions

title: Integrated DFT, molecular docking, and molecular dynamics investigation of some novel 2-thiohydantoin analogues as potent CDK2 inhibitors for anticancer therapy
task: Structure-activity prediction of 2-thiohydantoin CDK2 inhibitors
key_results: Compounds 2c, 2d, 2b showed binding affinities -9.312, -9.303, -9.269 kcal/mol (vs ATP -8.46); 2d optimal MM-PBSA ΔG_bind -34.50 ± 0.42 kcal/mol
comments: Computational predictions require experimental validation; MM-PBSA estimates are sensitive to force field and solvation assumptions.

hypotheses.hypothesis: 2-thiohydantoin derivatives with narrow HOMO-LUMO gaps are reactive CDK2 inhibitors
hypotheses.result: Compounds 2b-e showed 3.02–3.26 eV gaps and superior binding affinity

hypotheses.hypothesis: Key electrophilic centers C2, O12, C14 mediate protein interactions
hypotheses.result: QTAIM and Fukui analyses confirmed these sites; docking showed critical H-bonds with Lys33, Thr14

title: High-throughput adult Caenorhabditis elegans viability monitoring system
task: High-throughput automated viability monitoring of C. elegans
key_results: 95% live-count accuracy, 0.999 Pearson correlation with manual counts over 30 days, 4-5× throughput improvement
comments: Hardware-software integration addresses real practical bottlenecks in aging research, though validation limited to adult worms.

model_io.model: Integrated imaging system (collimated illumination + stepper motor + industrial cameras)

title: Temperature-dependent gating pathways in TRPV3
task: Temperature-dependent gating mechanisms in TRPV3 ion channel
key_results: Symmetric cold/heat activation below 30°C via Gibbs-Helmholtz equation; inactivation blocked above 30°C when lipid released by THCV; tetramer-to-pentamer transition during inactivation
comments: Heat capacity model validated for TRPV3; inactivation pathway reveals unexpected quaternary structure dynamics and lipid-dependent gating.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cold and heat activation of TRPV3 are symmetric and share a single thermosensitivity mechanism
hypotheses.result: Initial cold and heat-evoked open states showed similar thermosensitivity despite distinct structures; heat capacity model supported

hypotheses.hypothesis: Lipid binding at vanilloid site gates TRPV3 activation/inactivation below 30°C
hypotheses.result: THCV-induced inactivation only below 30°C; no inactivation above 30°C when lipid released

title: Classification of anterior cruciate ligament injury profiles through running analysis: a machine learning approach
task: Machine learning classification of ACL injury status from gait spatiotemporal features
key_results: KNN achieved 0.98 balanced accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score; AUC 0.99; stride/stance/swing time and A-P velocity most predictive
comments: Strong gait-based biomarkers identified; temporal features outperform spatial, aligning with known post-ACL kinematic changes.

model_io.model: KNN, GBM, XGBoost, RF, SVM, MLP classifiers

hypotheses.hypothesis: Post-ACL reconstruction gait changes are detectable by ML from treadmill kinematics
hypotheses.result: KNN identified subtle differences with 0.98 balanced accuracy; temporal gait variables most critical

title: Profiling histone post-translational modifications to identify signatures of epigenetic drug response in T-cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia
task: Histone PTM profiling to identify epigenetic drug response signatures in T-ALL
key_results: Distinct hPTM signatures linked to sensitivity for each drug class (HDACi, DNMTi, anthracyclines); in vitro signatures poorly predict in vivo (PDX) responses
comments: MS-based PTM profiling is sensitive; substantial in vitro-in vivo discrepancy highlights model selection importance for biomarker development.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Baseline histone PTM profiles predict in vitro drug sensitivity
hypotheses.result: Distinct signatures identified for each drug class; substantial in vitro-in vivo discrepancies observed

title: Exploring the limits of pre-trained embeddings in machine-guided protein design: a case study on predicting AAV vector viability
task: Systematic evaluation of pre-trained protein embeddings for AAV vector viability prediction
key_results: Amino acid-level embeddings outperform sequence-level in supervised tasks before fine-tuning; sequence-level optimal after fine-tuning; task-specific fine-tuning essential for sparse mutations
comments: Fine-tuning critically improves performance; dense embeddings require extent of sequence variation exceeding typical bioengineering studies.

model_io.model: ProtBERT and ESM2 embedding variants (amino acid and sequence-level)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Amino acid-level representations capture localized sequence signals better than global sequence embeddings
hypotheses.result: Amino acid embeddings outperformed sequence-level in supervised tasks before fine-tuning

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fine-tuning with task-specific labels compensates for limited bioengineering sequence variation
hypotheses.result: Optimal performance achieved with fine-tuned sequence-level embeddings despite sparse mutations

title: Immunoinformatic-based design of a multi-epitope subunit vaccine against Ruminococcus torques using subtractive proteomics and molecular dynamics simulations
task: Multi-epitope vaccine design against Ruminococcus torques
key_results: GC content 49.29%, CAI 0.875 indicating high expression potential; vaccine-TLR4 complex stable by MD simulation
comments: In silico design lacks experimental immunogenicity and protective efficacy validation; clinical translation requires wet-lab confirmation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: FtsX, single-stranded DNA binding protein, and FtsW are strong immunogenic targets
hypotheses.result: Identified as final target candidates by proteomics screening

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-epitope vaccine with TLR4 adjuvant induces stable immune response
hypotheses.result: MD simulations confirmed vaccine-TLR4 complex stability; requires experimental validation

title: LogoXpertNet: a novel lightweight logo classification using deep learning
task: Lightweight deep learning architecture for logo classification
key_results: Near-saturation accuracy on FlickrLogos-32, BelgaLogos, WebLogo-2M benchmarks; low computational overhead maintained
comments: Benchmark saturation limits interpretability of performance claims; real-world generalization to novel logos remains unclear.

model_io.model: LogoXpertNet (MobileNetV3 + SE blocks + CLFF + HSE-SAB + FA-CBAM)

title: Multimodal system for automated medical documentation and clinical decision support integrating contact center solutions
task: Automated medical documentation and clinical decision support system
key_results: All tested components achieved >80% effectiveness threshold; applicable to internal medicine and pediatrics
comments: Functional tests demonstrate high performance; clinical impact and user adoption rates not detailed.

title: Deep learning-based precision phenotyping of spine curvature identifies novel genetic risk loci for scoliosis in the UK Biobank
task: Deep learning-based spine curvature phenotyping from DXA imaging for scoliosis GWAS
key_results: Automated curvature correlated 0.83 with clinical Cobb angle; GWAS identified 2 novel loci (SEM1/SHFM1, chr3 lncRNA); outperformed case-control GWAS with 10× larger sample
comments: Quantitative imaging phenotypes capture genetic signal missed by categorical case-control labels; validates precision phenotyping for genomic discovery.

model_io.model: Deep learning vertebral segmentation + landmarking (U-Net or SegNet)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Quantitative imaging phenotypes identify more genome-wide significant loci than categorical case-control datasets
hypotheses.result: Identified 2 novel loci vs. case-control study with 10× sample size

hypotheses.hypothesis: Novel scoliosis loci in SEM1/SHFM1 and chr3 lncRNA (EDEM1-GRM7) are functionally relevant
hypotheses.result: Loci identified by quantitative GWAS; functional mechanisms require downstream investigation

title: A computational mechanism linking momentary craving and decision-making in alcohol drinkers and cannabis users
task: Computational model of craving-learning interaction in substance-related decision-making
key_results: Momentary craving biased substance-specific learning rate (opposite directions in alcohol vs. cannabis); model-derived parameters predicted alcohol, not cannabis, addiction risk
comments: Computational approach disentangles craving-choice coupling mechanisms; selective predictive utility suggests domain-specific clinical utility.

model_io.model: Reinforcement learning task (addictive cues vs. money) + craving questionnaires
Predictive regression (model parameters → addiction risk scores)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Momentary craving and reinforcement learning bi-directionally influence each other
hypotheses.result: Craving biased learning rate (opposite directions by substance); outcomes influenced craving

hypotheses.hypothesis: Model-derived parameters improve addiction risk prediction vs. baseline measures
hypotheses.result: Parameters predicted alcohol, but not cannabis, addiction risk scores

title: Artificial intelligence for early detection and risk prediction of antimicrobial resistance in aquatic ecosystems
task: AI framework for early detection and risk prediction of antimicrobial resistance in aquatic ecosystems
key_results: Integrated omics, environmental, and hydrological data into spatiotemporal predictive models for real-time AMR monitoring
comments: Vision paper outlining potential of One Health AMR surveillance; requires implementation of governance, ethics, and capacity-building frameworks.

title: Video-based cattle behaviour detection for digital twin development in precision dairy systems
task: Video-based behavior detection and tracking of individual cattle for dairy digital twins
key_results: TimeSformer achieved 85.0% accuracy (macro-F1 0.84) on 7 behaviors; 22.6 fps real-time throughput; attention on anatomically relevant regions (head/muzzle for feeding; limbs for postures)
comments: End-to-end pipeline (YOLOv11 + ByteTrack + TimeSformer) provides interpretable per-animal activity streams; attention visualizations validate biological plausibility.

model_io.model: YOLOv11 cow detection + ByteTrack identity persistence

inputs: Tracked cow video clips (7 behavior classes)

hypotheses.hypothesis: TimeSformer outperforms SlowFast for dairy behavior recognition under commercial conditions
hypotheses.result: 85.0% accuracy (macro-F1 0.84) with real-time 22.6 fps throughput

hypotheses.hypothesis: Model attention concentrates on task-relevant anatomical regions
hypotheses.result: Visualizations showed head/muzzle attention for feeding/drinking; torso/limbs for postures

title: An adaptive multi-scale spatio-temporal graph network for robust MOOC dropout prediction
task: MOOC student dropout prediction with multi-scale spatio-temporal networks
key_results: MST-GCN outperforms baselines on KDD Cup 2015 and XuetangX benchmarks; learns interpretable policy: long-term history for at-risk identification, short-term momentum for success prediction
comments: Novel adaptive gating mechanism is interpretable and shows strong empirical results, but evaluation limited to two datasets; generalization to other educational platforms unclear.

hypotheses.hypothesis: First-order temporal dependencies insufficient for capturing complex student behavior
hypotheses.result: Multi-scale fusion of short- and long-term memories via adaptive gating improves dropout prediction accuracy

hypotheses.hypothesis: Model exhibits robustness in unstructured, self-paced learning environments
hypotheses.result: Demonstrated superior performance on both structured (KDD Cup 2015) and self-paced (XuetangX) learning scenarios

title: Adaptive intelligent controller for a lower limb rehabilitation robot using QAOA-based online membership optimization
task: Fuzzy membership function optimization for lower-limb rehabilitation robot control
key_results: QAOA-optimized fuzzy controller reduces integral squared error by 96–99%, settling time by 62–63%, peak overshoot by 75–80% versus conventional methods
comments: Hardware-in-the-Loop validation strengthens claims, but quantum hardware requirements limit practical deployment; comparison against pure RL baselines would strengthen novelty claims.

hypotheses.hypothesis: QAOA-based online membership optimization improves trajectory tracking robustness
hypotheses.result: Significant reduction in ISE, settling time, and overshoot across nonlinear coupled joint systems with disturbances

hypotheses.hypothesis: Lyapunov stability guaranteed with faster energy dissipation
hypotheses.result: Energy-based analysis confirms enhanced closed-loop stability with improved convergence rates

title: Secure and intelligent SDN-IoV framework with blockchain-based authentication and optimization-based QoS routing
task: SDN-IoV secure routing and QoS management with blockchain authentication
key_results: ItFO-based multi-objective routing optimizes end-to-end delay, residual energy, link reliability, and security factors; blockchain prevents malicious intrusions with decentralized trust
comments: Framework is comprehensive but lacks concrete performance metrics or comparison benchmarks; claims about security and QoS improvement need quantitative validation on real or large-scale simulated networks.

model_io.model: Blockchain-enabled SDN-IoV Framework with Iterative Fossa Optimization
inputs: task metadata (deadline, data size, speed, emergency flag)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Blockchain-based authentication prevents malicious intrusions
hypotheses.result: Decentralized trust management enables secure vehicular communication without centralized authority

hypotheses.hypothesis: Priority-based request management improves QoS and energy efficiency
hypotheses.result: ItFO optimization considering multi-objective functions enhances delay, energy, reliability, and security trade-offs

title: Investigating expectations and needs regarding the use of large language models at Bavarian university clinics
task: Survey analysis of LLM adoption attitudes in clinical settings
key_results: 120 participants across 5 Bavarian clinics; majority use LLMs for research/summarization but report institutional unpreparedness; concerns include opacity, data privacy, and patient trust loss
comments: Statistically underpowered sample size for broad institutional conclusions; valuable for informing local adoption strategies but lacks generalizability to non-German healthcare systems.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Medical professionals perceive positive impact of LLMs on their field
hypotheses.result: Most participants believe LLMs will positively influence their field and enable personalized, evidence-based, cost-effective treatment

hypotheses.hypothesis: Institutions are prepared for LLM adoption
hypotheses.result: Participants overwhelmingly report institutions are not well-prepared, requiring education, training, infrastructure, and legal compliance improvements

title: DNA affects the phenotype of fuel-dependent coacervate droplets
task: Demonstrating genotype-phenotype coupling in fuel-dependent peptide-RNA coacervate synthetic cells via DNA sequence libraries
key_results: Identified strong preferences for guanine-rich and adenine-rich DNA motifs; adenine-rich sequences shorten droplet lifetimes via hybridization; guanine-rich sequences kinetically trap droplets altering morphology
comments: Elegant demonstration of genotype-driven selection in minimal synthetic systems; critical step toward Darwinian evolution in protocells but evolutionary dynamics remain undemonstrated.

model_io.model: Fuel-dependent peptide-RNA coacervate droplet with DNA genotype library

hypotheses.hypothesis: DNA sequences partition preferentially in coacervate droplets with motif-dependent preferences
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; guanine-rich and adenine-rich sequences show strong partition preferences

hypotheses.hypothesis: Partitioned DNA sequences alter synthetic cell phenotype via hybridization and peptide interactions
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; adenine-rich shorten lifetimes, guanine-rich kinetically trap droplets

title: Scalable foundation interatomic potentials via message-passing pruning and graph partitioning
task: Accelerating and scaling atomistic foundation models via message-passing pruning and GPU graph partitioning
key_results: Pruning reduces AFM parameters while preserving accuracy and data-efficiency; GPU-distributed strategy enables million-atom simulations at nanosecond timescales with AFM-level accuracy
comments: Practical workflow addressing critical scalability bottleneck of foundation models; substantial acceleration achieved but comprehensive benchmark comparisons to conventional potentials lacking.

model_io.model: Message-passing atomistic foundation model (AFM) with pruning and graph partitioning

hypotheses.hypothesis: Removing low-contribution message-passing layers preserves AFM accuracy while reducing parameters
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; pruning significantly reduces parameter count without accuracy loss

hypotheses.hypothesis: Graph partitioning enables efficient multi-GPU distribution for large-scale MD simulations
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; million-atom simulations on single and multiple GPUs demonstrated with AFM accuracy

title: Rational design of polymeric dielectrics guided by insightful understanding of electron transfer/transport in aperiodic systems
task: Rational design of polymeric dielectrics via computational-experimental study of electron transfer/transport in aperiodic systems
key_results: Energy barrier and spatial confinement of unoccupied frontier orbitals govern electron transfer; quantum current structure-dependence regulates conduction; three ab-initio descriptor design principle validated
comments: First quantitative real-space orbital analysis in aperiodic polymers; bridges microscopic quantum dynamics and macroscopic dielectric performance effectively.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Frontier orbital properties predict and regulate electron transfer in aperiodic systems
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; energy barrier and spatial confinement are experimentally characterizable and predictive

hypotheses.hypothesis: Chemical modification of frontier orbitals enables precise dielectric performance regulation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ab-initio descriptor-guided design achieved enhanced dielectric performance

title: Influence of programming-pulse properties on weight-update characteristics of charge-trapping IGZO synaptic transistors
task: Characterizing weight-update dynamics in charge-trapping IGZO synaptic transistors as function of programming pulse width
key_results: Maximum dynamic ratio of 18.5 achieved at PPW=3s; higher pulse widths increase dynamic ratio but accelerated recovery at 6s degrades performance
comments: Device-level optimization study relevant to neuromorphic computing; practical findings support analog AI accelerator design but lacks broader theoretical framework.

hypotheses.hypothesis: PPW affects weight-update characteristics via balance of energy per pulse and recovery rate
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; PPW=3s optimizes trade-off between pulse energy and recovery effects

hypotheses.hypothesis: Increased PPW with constant recovery improves dynamic ratio
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; higher energy per pulse from longer PPW enhances dynamic range

title: Nonlinear vibration behavior of self-sustaining CNT nanobeams under thermo-magnetic fields: surface energy insights for advanced sports applications
task: Modeling nonlinear vibrations of CNT nanobeams under thermo-magnetic fields for sports applications
key_results: Strategic adjustment of damping, crystal orientation, and geometric nonlinearity modifies frequency-amplitude behavior for customized rigidity and attenuation
comments: Timely focus on nanomaterial applications in sports; surface energy effects properly incorporated but numerical results and quantitative performance metrics are underspecified.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Surface effects and residual stresses significantly impact nanobeam dynamics at high surface-to-volume ratios
hypotheses.result: Implicitly confirmed via analysis framework; elevated surface elasticity improves shock absorption

hypotheses.hypothesis: Geometric nonlinearity and damping can be tuned for sports-specific material performance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; parametric adjustments enable customized frequency-amplitude behavior

title: Structural, optical and luminescence properties of Fe3+-doped mixed alkali zirconia-borate glasses for warm orange-red photonic applications
task: Designing Fe³⁺-doped mixed alkali zirconia-borate glasses for warm-emitting photonic applications
key_results: Bandgap reduced from 3.90 to 3.10 eV (direct) and 3.44 to 2.77 eV (indirect); orange-red luminescence at 550–570 nm; nonlinear refractive index increased
comments: Comprehensive spectroscopic characterization; CIE chromaticity suitable for warm LEDs but limited photoluminescence quantum yield data provided.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fe³⁺ doping reduces bandgap and introduces non-bridging ortho-borate units
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; density and structural modifications enable bandgap reduction

hypotheses.hypothesis: Increased refractive index enhances third-order nonlinearity
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; refractive index increase correlates with higher nonlinear refractive index

title: Quasi-integrability from $$\mathcal{P}\mathcal{T}$$-symmetry
task: Establishing 𝒫𝒯-symmetry as fundamental origin of quasi-integrability in deformed systems
key_results: 𝒫𝒯-symmetric phase ensures asymptotic conservation of quasi-conserved charges; demonstrated for KdV, NLSE, and non-local NLSE
comments: Elegant theoretical framework connecting symmetry to integrability-breaking; lacks explicit numerical verification of conservation laws in realistic perturbation regimes.

hypotheses.hypothesis: 𝒫𝒯-symmetry is the natural cause of quasi-integrability in deformed models
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 𝒫𝒯-phase ensures definite properties of Lax pair and anomalous contribution

hypotheses.hypothesis: Abelianization preserves 𝒫𝒯-behavior of charge densities
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; quasi-deformed charges acquire definite 𝒫𝒯-properties suitable for asymptotic conservation

title: Adaptive governance in collective risk social dilemmas
task: Optimizing cooperation in repeated social dilemmas via adaptive governance
key_results: Moderate target increases promote cooperation at low risk; higher targets sustain cooperation at high risk; reward adjustments expand cooperation basins at low risk
comments: Game-theoretic approach to collective action problems; practical application to policy design but limited experimental validation.

model_io.model: Repeated game with dynamic target adjustment

hypotheses.hypothesis: Adjusting collective targets dynamically improves cooperation compared to static targets
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; target adjustment is crucial for promoting cooperation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Risk level determines optimal target elevation strategy
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; moderate targets effective at low risk, higher targets at high risk

title: High-entropy design of transition metal oxide semiconductors with ultra-low thermal conductivity
task: Engineering semiconducting high-entropy metal oxides with ultra-low thermal conductivity
key_results: Achieved thermal conductivity <1 W/m/K in high-entropy wolframite A₆WO₄; demonstrated insulator-to-semiconductor transition via cation mixing
comments: Well-characterized mechanisms (electronic and vibrational) underlying performance; highly relevant for thermoelectric applications.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Configurational entropy drives insulating oxides into semiconducting state
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; cation mixing activates in-gap states and increases crystal field splitting

hypotheses.hypothesis: Ultra-low thermal conductivity arises from phonon disorder scattering
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; phonon-defect and momentum non-conserving three-phonon scattering dominate

title: Achieving wideband wavefront manipulation in asymmetric media by phase-engineered metasurfaces with near-unity transmission
task: Designing wideband gradient Huygens' metasurfaces with near-unity transmission in asymmetric media
key_results: Achieved ~13% relative X-band bandwidth with near-unity transmission across 2π phase coverage; demonstrated wideband beam steering and energy focusing
comments: Cascaded design integrating dielectric spacers as key degree of freedom; substantial improvement over prior approaches with practical applications in NDT and sensing.

model_io.model: Gradient Huygens' metasurface with dielectric spacers

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dielectric spacers harmonize resonance and propagation phases for wideband impedance matching
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; cascaded design achieves simultaneous impedance matching and wavefront transformation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Near-unity transmission enables efficient asymmetric wavefront manipulation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; wideband beam steering and focusing demonstrated numerically and experimentally
